§6Aromatic Hydrocarbons§f

§aA key chemical feedstock from cracked oil refining — essential for bakelite, TNT, solvent, and advanced chains!§f

Aromatic Hydrocarbons are a flammable, viscous liquid produced by refining §bHot Cracked Oil§f in the §bOil Refinery§f. Every §e1,000mB§f yields §e150mB§f of Aromatic Hydrocarbons as the least common but most versatile output.

§6Production§f:
§b• Oil Refinery§f (Hot Cracked Oil): §e150mB§f per §e1,000mB§f.
§b• Oil Refinery§f (Desulfurized Hot Cracked Oil): §e150mB§f per §e1,000mB§f.
§b• Catalytic Reformer§f (Light Oil / Cracked Light Oil): §e500mB§f per §e1,000mB§f input.
§b• Fractioning Tower§f: From Coker Gas, Reformate, or BTX.
§b• Catalytic Cracking Tower§f: From Crude Oil, Bitumen, or BTX with steam.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Bakelite§f (Chemical Plant): §e500mB§f + §e500mB§f Petroleum Gas → §e1×§f Bakelite Bar.
§b• TNT§f (Chemical Plant): §e500mB§f + Niter → §e4×§f TNT.
§b• Solvent§f (Industrial Mixer): §e500mB§f + §e500mB§f Naphtha → §e1,000mB§f Solvent.
§b• Coker Unit§f: §e1,700mB§f → §e1×§f Petroleum Coke + §e170mB§f Coker Gas.
§b• Industrial Solidification§f: §e3,900mB§f → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.

§eTip§f: Aromatic Hydrocarbons are the cheapest source of the benzene ring structure in HBM. Route your cracked oil through the Oil Refinery specifically to produce this output — it gates access to bakelite, which is a polymer substitute that requires no coal or fluorite!
